prompt: What is the capital of Canada, and in which province is it located?
Who developed the theory of general relativity, and in what year was it published?
What causes the aurora borealis? Explain briefly in 2-3 sentences.
Why do commercial aircraft typically cruise at altitudes around 35,000 feet? Give the two main reasons briefly.
Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles, and in which years did it win them?
A jacket costs $80 and is on sale for 25% off. Sales tax of 8% is added after the discount. Calculate the final price.
A town of 45,000 people grows by 3% each year. Calculate the projected population after 5 years, rounded to the nearest hundred.
A company earned $2.4 million in revenue in 2023. Revenue grew 15% in 2024 and then declined 8% in 2025. How much revenue did the company earn in 2025?
A new car costs $32,000 and loses 18% of its value each year. After how many full years will the car be worth less than half its original price?
You invest $10,000 at 6% annual interest compounded monthly. How much will the investment be worth after 3 years, and how much more is that than the same account earning 6% simple interest?
Classify the sentiment of this review as Positive, Negative, or Neutral, and justify your label in one sentence: 'The pasta was decent but the 45-minute wait killed the mood.'
'I can't believe I waited a year for this update. Everything I loved about the app is gone.' — What is the sentiment of this comment? Give a one-line justification.
Determine the sentiment (Positive, Negative, or Neutral) and explain your reasoning in one sentence: 'Oh great, another meeting that could have been an email. Truly the highlight of my week.'

Label the overall sentiment of this hotel review and justify the label in one sentence: 'Stunning views and an unbeatable location, but the room smelled musty and the front desk seemed completely overwhelmed.'
What is the sentiment of this tweet — Positive, Negative, or Neutral? Justify in one sentence: 'The quarterly earnings report will be released Thursday at 9am ET.'

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: A 2024 survey of 3,000 knowledge workers found that employees with flexible remote options reported 22% higher job satisfaction and were 35% less likely to consider quitting than fully in-office peers. However, respondents under 25 reported feeling less connected to colleagues and mentors, and managers cited difficulty onboarding new hires remotely.

Summarize this passage in exactly 3 bullet points: Coral reefs occupy less than 1% of the ocean floor yet support roughly a quarter of all marine species. Rising sea temperatures trigger bleaching events, in which corals expel the symbiotic algae that provide most of their energy, leaving them white and vulnerable to starvation. Back-to-back bleaching events in 2016 and 2017 damaged two-thirds of the Great Barrier Reef. Scientists are experimenting with breeding heat-tolerant corals and shading reefs during heat waves, but most researchers agree these measures only buy time without steep cuts in greenhouse gas emissions.

Write a TL;DR of no more than 20 words: The Lumen 3 smart thermostat launches next month at $129. It learns household schedules within a week, integrates with all major voice assistants, and the company claims average heating-bill savings of 18%. Preorders open Friday, and early buyers receive two extra room sensors at no charge.

Summarize this study in exactly two sentences — the first stating the findings, the second the limitations: Researchers tracked 812 adults for six months and found that those who walked at least 7,000 steps daily had 31% fewer sick days and reported better sleep quality than sedentary participants. The study relied on self-reported sleep data, drew participants from a single city, and could not rule out that healthier people simply walk more.

Condense these meeting notes into a summary with exactly two labeled sections, 'Decisions:' and 'Action items:', each 1-2 lines: The product team met Tuesday to review Q3 priorities. After long debate, the group agreed to delay the mobile redesign to Q4 and ship the payments integration first. Dana will draft the revised roadmap by Friday. Miguel agreed to contact the payments vendor about sandbox access. The team also decided to move weekly standups to 9:30am. Priya raised concerns about testing capacity but no decision was made.

Extract the named entities from this sentence as a JSON array of objects with keys "text" and "label" (labels: person, org, location, date): 'Sundar Pichai opened Google I/O in Mountain View on May 14, 2024.'

Identify all entities in the following text and return a JSON array of {"text": ..., "label": ...} objects using labels person/org/location/date: 'The WHO and Doctors Without Borders coordinated relief efforts in Port-au-Prince after the January 12 earthquake, spokesperson Marie Dupont confirmed.'

Extract every named entity from this text into a JSON array with fields "text" and "label" (person/org/location/date): 'Jordan flew from Amazon's Seattle headquarters to Jordan on March 3, 2025, to meet officials from Chase.'

List all named entities with their labels (person, org, location, date) as a JSON array of {"text", "label"} objects: 'On 4 July 1776, the Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence in Philadelphia; Thomas Jefferson drafted most of it.'

Extract all named entities (labels: person, org, location, date) and return them as a JSON array of {"text", "label"} objects: 'At CES in Las Vegas on January 7, 2025, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled new chips built with TSMC, while rival AMD, led by Lisa Su, scheduled its own event in Austin for January 9.'

Fix the bug in this Python function — it should sum the first n elements of nums:
```python
def sum_first_n(nums, n):
    total = 0
    for i in range(1, n):
        total += nums[i]
    return total
```

This Python function behaves strangely when called multiple times. Find and fix the bug:
```python
def add_item(item, items=[]):
    items.append(item)
    return items
```

Logic puzzle: Priya, Marcus, and Elena each commute by a different mode: bike, bus, or car. Priya refuses to use anything with a motor. Marcus sits in traffic jams every morning driving alone. Deduce who uses which mode of transport.
Four presentations (A, B, C, D) must be scheduled in four consecutive slots. A must go first. D must come immediately after A. C must be earlier than B. Deduce the only possible order.

A classic knights and knaves puzzle: on an island, knights always tell the truth and knaves always lie. You meet Zed and Ora. Zed says: 'We are both knaves.' Determine what Zed and Ora each are, and explain why.

Puzzle: Four runners — Kim, Raj, Tom, and Uma — finished a race in distinct positions 1 through 4. Kim finished ahead of Tom but behind Uma. Uma was not first. Raj did not finish last. Determine the exact finishing order.

Logic puzzle: Ana, Ben, Cara, Dev, and Eli sit in a row of five seats numbered 1 to 5, left to right. Ana sits at one of the ends. Dev sits in seat 3. Ben sits immediately to the right of Cara. Ana sits somewhere to the right of Ben. Deduce the full seating arrangement.
Write a Python function is_palindrome(s) that returns True if s is a palindrome, ignoring case, spaces, and punctuation.
Implement a Python function merge_intervals(intervals) that takes a list of [start, end] pairs and returns the merged, sorted list. Example: [[1,3],[2,6],[8,10]] should return [[1,6],[8,10]].
Write a SQL query that returns the names of the top 5 customers by total order amount, given tables customers(id, name) and orders(id, customer_id, amount).
Write a JavaScript function debounce(fn, delay) that returns a debounced version of fn which only executes after delay milliseconds have passed since the last call.
Implement an LRUCache class in Python with get(key) and put(key, value) methods that both run in O(1) time, evicting the least recently used entry when capacity is exceeded.
